result.problem_goal: Find the measure of angle DOB

givens.fact: The center of the circle is O
Points A, B, D, C are all points on the circle
Point E appears to be outside the circle on a line through B and C

steps.content: BCE is a straight line, since DCE is 64° BCD is 116°
ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral thus angle BAD is also 64°
this is because of the opposite angle sum of cyclic quadrilaterals = 180°
Line BD makes the triangle ABD and since the angle BAD made by the segment is half of the angle BOD made by the center

result.summary: The student correctly solved the problem using properties of cyclic quadrilaterals and the central angle theorem. The reasoning chain is valid: BCE is a straight line making angle BCD = 116°, opposite angles in cyclic quadrilateral ABCD sum to 180° giving angle BAD = 64°, and the central angle BOD is twice the inscribed angle BAD, yielding 128°.

givens.fact: The center of the circle is O
Points A, B, D, C are all points on the circle
Point E appears to be outside the circle on a line through B and C

derived_facts: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral (all four points lie on the circle)
BCE appears to be a straight line (B, C, E are collinear)
If BCE is a straight line, then angle BCD + angle DCE = 180°, so angle BCD = 180° - 64° = 116°
In a cyclic quadrilateral, opposite angles sum to 180°, so angle BAD = 180° - 116° = 64°
The central angle theorem states that a central angle is twice the inscribed angle subtending the same arc
Angle BOD (central angle) = 2 × angle BAD (inscribed angle) when both subtend arc BD

steps.content: BCE is a straight line, since DCE is 64° BCD is 116°
ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral thus angle BAD is also 64°
this is because of the opposite angle sum of cyclic quadrilaterals = 180°
Line BD makes the triangle ABD and since the angle BAD made by the segment is half of the angle BOD made by the center
